ip_outcome_note: Confirmed Voltorb via card name, PokemonTCG.io API (swsh2-56, National Pokedex no. 100, Lightning type), artist Hasegawa Saki, and canonical red-and-white spherical design with angry eyes.

trivia_bullets.text: Voltorb is National Pokedex no. 100, first discovered at the facility where modern Poke Balls were manufactured. Pokedex entries describe it as possibly created when a Poke Ball was exposed to an energy pulse. Its non-naturally-occurring components support the manufactured-object-become-creature reading. Strongest industrial-design narrative in the corpus: a factory product that gained volatile life.
sources: Bulbapedia: Voltorb via WebSearch snippet; PokemonTCG.io API: swsh2-56

trivia_bullets.text: Community analysis notes Voltorb may be based on tsukumogami — inanimate objects in Japanese folklore that acquire a spirit upon their 100th year. Voltorb's National Pokedex number is exactly 100, likely an intentional encoding of this mythological threshold.
sources: Wikipedia: Voltorb; Bulbapedia: Voltorb; via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Pokemon Legends: Arceus (2022) introduced Hisuian Voltorb, resembling Apricorn-made Poke Balls (pre-industrial era). This opens the design question: did pre-Hisuian Voltorbs inspire Poke Ball design, or are Kantonian Voltorbs mimicking modern Poke Balls? The games leave the question intentionally open.
sources: GameRant: 'Hisuian Voltorb is a Chicken or Egg Conundrum'; CBR: Hisuian Voltorb; via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: 'Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poke Ball, it has zapped many people.' Power plant habitat ties the species directly to the industrial-electric-infrastructure setting.

related_cards.name: Electrode
related_cards.note: evolution of Voltorb, not in corpus

related_cards.name: Hisuian Voltorb
related_cards.note: regional alternate form, Apricorn-ball design, not in corpus
